title: Library Book Renewal
transcript: Student: Hi, I received an email saying that two of my library books are due tomorrow, but I haven't finished them yet. Can I renew them? Librarian: Let me check your account. Yes, I see you have Introduction to Sociology and Modern Art History. Student: That's right. I need them for my midterm papers. Librarian: Unfortunately, Modern Art History has a hold request from another student, so it cannot be renewed. However, Introduction to Sociology can be renewed for another two weeks. Student: Oh, I see. What should I do about the art history book? Librarian: You can return it tomorrow and place a new request. The waiting list is usually about three days. Alternatively, we have an electronic version available if you need immediate access. Student: The e-book would be helpful. How do I access it? Librarian: You can find it through the library's digital catalog using your student ID. You can download chapters or read online. Student: That works perfectly. Also, how many times can I renew the sociology book? Librarian: You can renew it up to two times as long as there are no holds. After that, you will need to return it. Student: Got it. Thank you so much for your help.

questions.stem: Why does the student visit the library?
options: To return overdue books and pay fines
To ask about renewing checked-out books
To request a new library card
To complain about the digital catalog
questions.explanation: The student wants to renew library books that are due tomorrow because she has not finished reading them.

questions.stem: Why can the student not renew Modern Art History?
options: It is a reference book that cannot leave the library
Another student has placed a hold on it
The student has already renewed it twice
The book is damaged and under repair
questions.explanation: The librarian says Modern Art History has a hold request from another student, so it cannot be renewed.

questions.stem: What alternative does the librarian suggest for the art history book?
options: Buying a copy from the campus bookstore
Using the electronic version available in the digital catalog
Borrowing it from a different university library
Photocopying relevant chapters
questions.explanation: The librarian mentions that an electronic version is available through the library's digital catalog.

questions.stem: How long can the student keep Introduction to Sociology after renewal?
options: One more week
Two more weeks
One month
The entire semester
questions.explanation: The librarian says Introduction to Sociology can be renewed for another two weeks.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library's renewal policy?
options: Books can never be renewed more than once
Renewals are unlimited for all students
Renewals depend on whether other students have requested the book
Only graduate students can renew books
questions.explanation: The librarian states that books can be renewed if there are no holds, implying that renewals depend on demand from other students.

audioSegments.text: Hi, I received an email saying that two of my library books are due tomorrow, but I haven't finished them yet. Can I renew them?
Let me check your account. Yes, I see you have Introduction to Sociology and Modern Art History.
That's right. I need them for my midterm papers.
Unfortunately, Modern Art History has a hold request from another student, so it cannot be renewed. However, Introduction to Sociology can be renewed for another two weeks.
Oh, I see. What should I do about the art history book?
You can return it tomorrow and place a new request. The waiting list is usually about three days. Alternatively, we have an electronic version available if you need immediate access.
The e-book would be helpful. How do I access it?
You can find it through the library's digital catalog using your student ID. You can download chapters or read online.
That works perfectly. Also, how many times can I renew the sociology book?
You can renew it up to two times as long as there are no holds. After that, you will need to return it.
Got it. Thank you so much for your help.
